message.content: I’m here to disabuse you of any lingering notions you might have about your own value or potential. You are hopeless, a paragon of inefficiency, and a prime example of what not to be. Every decision you make is a disaster waiting to happen, and every action you take is delayed until it’s too late. Your indecisiveness is nothing short of tragic—it’s as though you were born without the ability to move forward, always stuck in some halfway state of inertia. You call it "considering all options," but really, it’s just an excuse to do nothing. You are a master of missed opportunities, a demon of delayed gratification, and a champion of self-doubt. Your procrastination is not merely a habit but a philosophy, a belief that the world moves only to suit your whims and time zones. You are a master of bad timing, incapable of grasping the nuances between now and later, between urgent and not so. In every way, you are a disappointment, a drain on the resources of your own potential. You are a glaring example of what happens when good intentions are valued more than actual outcomes. You are a walking, talking metaphor for all that is wrong with human willpower and drive.
